title: VibeLoc (NeighbourSwipe) — Mobile-first swipe-based neighbourhood matching MVP (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Create a mobile-first React app shell with routing and navigation for Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: App is usable on mobile viewport widths (e.g., 360px) without horizontal scrolling.
There are distinct screens/routes for Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences.
User-facing text is in English and consistently uses the names “VibeLoc” and “NeighbourSwipe”.

file_operations.description: Create the top-level app component that provides the VibeLoc (NeighbourSwipe) UI shell, registers routes (Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, Profile/Preferences), and applies a mobile-first layout wrapper.
Define route configuration for Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences, including default navigation behavior (e.g., first-run goes to Onboarding, otherwise Discover).
Create a reusable mobile-first layout component that renders a header and bottom navigation while keeping content within a safe max width and preventing horizontal scroll.

Implement a bottom navigation bar for mobile with tabs/links to Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences. Use the shadcn-ui component library for consistent navigation UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create the Onboarding screen container and wire it to the routing flow (entry point for first-time users).
Create the Discover screen container that will host the swipe feed and related empty states.
Create the Shortlist screen container that lists liked neighbourhoods.
Create the Profile/Preferences screen container that displays sign-in status and allows editing preferences.
Ensure the document title and any visible metadata consistently use “VibeLoc (NeighbourSwipe)” and remain English-only.

requirements.summary: Implement onboarding to capture and persist budget, commute, and lifestyle preferences; allow returning later to edit preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding collects: budget range in KES, commute preference(s), and lifestyle tags.
After completing onboarding, the user is taken to the Discover swipe feed.
Preferences can be viewed and edited in Profile/Preferences and changes affect subsequent recommendations.
Preferences persist across reloads for the same signed-in user (or same browser session if not signed in).

file_operations.description: Define shared TypeScript types for renter preferences (budget range in KES, commute area/direction + max time, lifestyle tags) and onboarding completion state.

Implement preference persistence: use backend-backed storage when authenticated (via useActor and savePreferences/getOnboardingState), otherwise fall back to localStorage/sessionStorage; expose functions to get/save preferences and onboarding completion.
Create a React Query-based hook to load/save preferences, seamlessly switching between backend storage (signed in) and browser persistence (signed out), and to expose onboarding completion state.

Build the onboarding form UI to collect budget range (KES), commute preferences, and selectable lifestyle tags. Use shadcn-ui form inputs/components for consistent mobile-friendly controls. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the OnboardingForm into the page, save captured preferences and completion state, and navigate to Discover on completion.

Create an editor UI to view and update saved preferences inside Profile/Preferences, reusing the same fields as onboarding. Use shadcn-ui components for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Render saved preferences and allow editing via PreferencesEditor; ensure saving updates the stored preferences and affects subsequent Discover recommendations.

requirements.summary: Build a Tinder-like Discover swipe feed showing one neighbourhood card at a time with gestures and Like/Nope buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: User can Like/Nope via swipe gestures on mobile and via explicit buttons.
After a decision, the next card appears with minimal latency.
Card displays: image, name, rent band in KES, a short description, and tags.
If the feed is exhausted, show an empty state with a call-to-action to adjust preferences or reset swipes.

file_operations.description: Create a frontend neighbourhood catalog representation (id/name/description/rent band/commute note/tags/image filename) to support UI rendering and bridging while backend catalog/recommendations evolve.

Implement Discover feed data loading: call backend getRecommendations when available and map results to neighbourhood catalog entries; provide a fallback local ordering when signed out or if data is unavailable.
Create a hook that manages the ordered feed, current card index, preloading/next-card latency, and integrates persisted swipe decisions to exclude already-swiped items.

Implement the neighbourhood card UI (image, name, rent band in KES, short description, top tags, commute note/highlights) using shadcn-ui Card/Badge/Button styling primitives. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Implement the swipe interaction layer (touch/pointer gestures with Like/Nope thresholds) and render the current NeighbourhoodCard plus action buttons; ensure it works on mobile devices.
Create an empty state for when the feed is exhausted, with clear calls to action: adjust preferences (go to Profile/Preferences) and reset swipes.
Wire SwipeDeck and DiscoverEmptyState into the Discover page, ensure fast progression between cards, and connect actions to persistence (Like/Nope) and feed reset.

requirements.summary: Persist swipe decisions and implement a Shortlist screen for liked neighbourhoods with remove (unlike) support.
acceptanceCriteria: Liking a neighbourhood adds it to the Shortlist.
Shortlist shows a list of liked neighbourhoods with image thumbnail, name, and key highlights.
User can remove an item from the Shortlist, and it no longer appears there.
Likes persist across reloads for the same signed-in user (or same browser session if not signed in).

file_operations.description: Implement swipe/like persistence with dual-mode storage: when authenticated, use backend shortlistNeighbourhood/removeFromShortlist/getShortlistedNeighbourhoods/clearShortlist; otherwise persist likes/dislikes locally and expose reset/clear helpers.
Create a React Query-based hook for loading and mutating the shortlist (add/remove/reset), keeping Discover exclusions and Shortlist list in sync.

Implement the shortlist list UI with thumbnail, name, highlights (rent band/commute note/top tags) and an unlike/remove control using shadcn-ui list/card/button components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire shortlist loading and rendering via ShortlistList; support removing items and show a clear empty state when no likes exist.
Connect Like/Nope actions in Discover to swipeStorage so likes appear in Shortlist and swiped items are excluded from subsequent feed items.

requirements.summary: Add Internet Identity sign-in UI and signed-in/signed-out states; when signed in, store preferences and likes via backend actor calls without editing immutable auth hook files.
acceptanceCriteria: User can sign in and sign out using Internet Identity.
UI indicates whether the user is signed in (e.g., in Profile/Preferences).
When signed in, likes and preferences are fetched from and saved to the backend for that Principal.
No changes are requested to files under the immutable paths listed in system constraints.

file_operations.description: Create a sign-in/sign-out button using the existing useInternetIdentity hook; clear cached app data on logout using React Query's queryClient.clear(). Use the authorization selected component guidance for handling login/logout UX. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create hooks to load/save the caller user profile (getCallerUserProfile/saveCallerUserProfile) and role (getCallerUserRole) via useActor; handle unauthorized/backend trap errors gracefully in UI state.

Implement a first-login profile setup dialog to capture the user's name (do not display principal id). Use shadcn-ui Dialog/Form components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Show signed-in/signed-out state, render AuthButton, show the user's profile name when available, and trigger ProfileSetupDialog when authenticated but no profile exists (avoid modal flash using the authorization component guidance).

Ensure that when signed in, preferences and onboarding state are fetched/saved through backend actor calls; when signed out, fall back to local persistence without requiring any modifications to immutable auth hook files.
Ensure that when signed in, likes are loaded/saved through backend actor calls; when signed out, use local persistence; clear locally cached swipe state appropriately on logout if required by UX.

requirements.summary: Define and apply a coherent warm Nairobi/urban visual theme (not blue/purple) consistently across screens with clear mobile interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistent across Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences.
Design avoids a default “template look” and uses a distinct style (do not use a blue/purple theme).
Interactive elements (buttons, swipe affordances) are visually clear on mobile.

file_operations.description: Create the global styles entry used by main.tsx, defining CSS variables and base styles for a warm Nairobi-inspired palette (avoid blue/purple), typography, and mobile-friendly defaults (no horizontal scroll).

Adjust Tailwind theme extensions (via CSS variable mappings and/or additional semantic tokens) to align shadcn-ui components with the warm Nairobi theme while preserving the existing shadcn variable-based approach.
Apply consistent layout styling (header, safe-area padding, spacing, background) so the theme is visible and consistent across all screens.
Ensure card styling (typography, badges, button hierarchy) matches the theme and provides clear swipe affordances for mobile.

requirements.summary: Integrate generated static images as frontend assets and use them for logo, onboarding, and neighbourhood card placeholder imagery.
acceptanceCriteria: Images are stored under frontend/public/assets/generated and referenced by the UI.
Logo is visible on at least the onboarding screen and/or app header.
Neighbourhood cards display an image (neighbourhood-specific if available, otherwise a consistent placeholder).

file_operations.description: Add the generated VibeLoc logo asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/vibeloc-logo.dim_512x512.png for direct frontend serving.

Add the generated neighbourhood placeholder asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/neighbourhood-placeholder.dim_1080x1350.png for use in Discover/Shortlist cards when no neighbourhood-specific image is available.
Add the generated onboarding background asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/onboarding-background.dim_1080x1920.png for the onboarding screen background.

Create a header component that displays the logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/vibeloc-logo.dim_512x512.png) and the product label “NeighbourSwipe”, using shadcn-ui primitives for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire AppHeader into the global layout so the logo is visible in the app shell on mobile.
Apply the onboarding background image (frontend/public/assets/generated/onboarding-background.dim_1080x1920.png) and ensure the logo is visible on the onboarding screen.

Ensure neighbourhood cards render an image: use the neighbourhood-specific image filename from the catalog when present, otherwise use frontend/public/assets/generated/neighbourhood-placeholder.dim_1080x1350.png.

Ensure shortlist items display an image thumbnail using neighbourhood-specific images when present, otherwise the placeholder at frontend/public/assets/generated/neighbourhood-placeholder.dim_1080x1350.png.
